1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Capture the active Claude session's JSONL transcript(s) into the repo for provenance.
3	
4	Run by the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so every commit pulls in an up-to-date
5	snapshot of the agent interactions/effort behind it — an auditable record alongside the work.
6	
7	What it captures: the *active* session (the most-recently-modified top-level transcript in the
8	Claude project dir) plus that session's ``<id>/`` subtree (subagents + workflow transcripts).
9	Other/unrelated sessions are left out. Credential-shaped secrets are redacted on the way in.
10	
11	Honest scope: transcripts still contain local filesystem paths and may contain the operator's
12	email and internal reasoning. If this repo is published, review/exclude ``transcripts/`` first
13	(see transcripts/README.md). The wheel never ships them (only ``src/assay_engine`` is packaged).
14	
15	Config: ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC overrides the source dir. Exits 0 on any error (never blocks a
16	commit).
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime as _dt
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	import os
25	import platform
26	import re
27	import socket
28	import sys
29	from pathlib import Path
30	
31	DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
32	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
33	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
34	
35	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
36	# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
37	# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
38	# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
39	# for the full public-release consideration.
40	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
41	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
42	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
43	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
44	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
45	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
46	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
47	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
48	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
49	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
50	    # password-class env vars (#B-12-1): PGPASSWORD, DATABASE_PASSWORD, DB_PASSWORD, DB_PASS,
51	    # REDIS_PASSWORD, etc. — absent from the _TOKEN/_KEY/_SECRET rules above. These appear in
52	    # `env`/`printenv` output and in psycopg error messages captured in agent transcripts.
53	    # Boundaries: _PASS\b avoids BYPASS/COMPASS; PGPASSWORD matched explicitly (ends in GPASSWORD,
54	    # not _PASSWORD). No minimum-length floor — DB_PASS=[REDACTED] is unambiguous credential context.
55	    re.compile(r'((?:PGPASSWORD|_PASSWORD|_PASS\b)"\s*:\s*")[^"]+?(")'),
56	    re.compile(r"((?:PGPASSWORD|_PASSWORD|_PASS\b)=)[^\s\"']+"),
57	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
58	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
59	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
60	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED]    # The Claude project-dir SLUG (e.g. -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc): redact the username segment
62	    # regardless of length (#H-018) — the bare-token rule below skips short handles to avoid
63	    # over-redacting common words, but the slug form is unambiguous PII at any length.
64	    re.compile(r"(-(?:Users|home)-)[^-\s/\"']+"),  # -Users-[REDACTED]- → -Users-[REDACTED]-
65	    # Private (RFC1918) IPv4 addresses (#P10-PII-1): captured ifconfig/route/lsof output leaks the
66	    # operator's LAN subnet/gateway/host IP (e.g. [REDACTED], gateway [REDACTED]) — infra-
67	    # identifying topology no other rule covers. Redact 10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16. Loopback
68	    # (127.0.0.0/8) and 0.0.0.0 are NOT private/identifying and are left (they appear in guard
69	    # tests/messages); public IPs (e.g. remote API endpoints) are not the operator's and are left.
70	    # Digit/dot lookarounds (not \b): the IP often abuts a JSON-escaped newline ("...\\n192.168...")
71	    # where \b fails because the escape's 'n' is a word char. Leading guard: not preceded by a
72	    # digit/dot (so we don't match inside a longer dotted number). Trailing guard: not followed by a
73	    # digit, and not by a dot-then-digit — this still bounds a longer IP ("[REDACTED]", "...0.134.5")
74	    # WITHOUT rejecting an IP that merely ends a sentence ("addr [REDACTED]. Next ...") — the
75	    # earlier (?![\d.]) refused the sentence-final period and left the operator's real IP in cleartext
76	    # (#P10-PII-1 confirm-concern).
77	    re.compile(
78	        r"(?<![\d.])(?:10\.\d{1,3}\.\d{1,3}\.\d{1,3}"
79	        r"|172\.(?:1[6-9]|2\d|3[01])\.\d{1,3}\.\d{1,3}"
80	        r"|192\.168\.\d{1,3}\.\d{1,3})(?!\d)(?!\.\d)"
81	    ),  # RFC1918 → [REDACTED]
82	    # IPv6 ULA (fd00::/8, fc00::/7) and link-local (fe80::/10) addresses (#B-11-1): captured
83	    # ifconfig/ip addr output leaks the operator's internal IPv6 topology just as RFC1918 does
84	    # for IPv4. ULA prefixes (fd/fc) identify the operator's private LAN; link-local (fe80)
85	    # exposes interface-specific context. Loopback (::1) starts with ':' and is NOT matched.
86	    # Zone IDs (%utun0, %lo0, %en0) are included so the full token is redacted.
87	    re.compile(
88	        r"(?<![0-9a-fA-F:])(?:fe80|f[cd][0-9a-fA-F]{2}):[0-9a-fA-F:]{2,}(?:%[a-zA-Z0-9]+)?",
89	        re.IGNORECASE,
90	    ),  # IPv6 ULA + link-local → [REDACTED]
91	    # MAC addresses (#B-11-2): ifconfig output contains 'ether [REDACTED]' — persistent
92	    # hardware identity for the primary interface (not randomized on wired macOS). Six colon-
93	    # separated 2-hex-digit groups; lookbehind/lookahead guard against partial matches inside
94	    # longer hex strings or IPv6 addresses (which use 4-digit groups, not 2-digit groups).
95	    re.compile(
96	        r"(?<![0-9a-fA-F])(?:[0-9a-fA-F]{2}:){5}[0-9a-fA-F]{2}(?![0-9a-fA-F:])",
97	        re.IGNORECASE,
98	    ),  # MAC address → [REDACTED]
99	]
100	
101	
102	def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
103	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
104	
105	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
106	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
107	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
108	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
109	    """
110	    try:
111	        name = Path.home().name
112	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
113	        return None
114	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
115	        return None
116	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
117	
118	
119	def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
120	    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
121	
122	    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
123	    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
124	    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
125	    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
126	    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
127	    over-redacting common words.
128	    """
129	    names: list[str] = []
130	    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
131	    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
132	    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
133	    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
134	    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
135	    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
136	    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
137	        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
138	        if v:
139	            names.append(v)
140	    try:
141	        import subprocess
142	
143	        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
144	            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
145	            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
146	                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
147	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
148	        pass
149	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
150	    seen: set[str] = set()
151	    for n in names:
152	        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
153	            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
154	        seen.add(n)
155	        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
156	        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
157	        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
158	        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
159	        # CASE-INSENSITIVE (#J-002 confirm-concern round 2): the operator's name also appears
160	        # lowercased as actor/handle tokens — "[REDACTED]@hcgrc" (an initiated_by field), "[REDACTED]-[REDACTED]
161	        # (human)" in actor tables — which a case-sensitive pattern misses. re.IGNORECASE covers all
162	        # casings; "Joneses"/"joneses" stays protected by the trailing \b either way.
163	        toks = n.split()
164	        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in toks) + r"\b", re.IGNORECASE))
165	        # Redact each NAME TOKEN standalone too (#J-002 confirm-concern): the full-name pattern
166	        # leaves the operator's bare first/last name in cleartext — e.g. "[REDACTED] approves ..." and
167	        # "[REDACTED] builds and governs ..." appear in governance prose hundreds of times, uniquely
168	        # re-identifying the operator. Per-token redaction (len>=4, trailing \b, no leading \b for
169	        # the JSON-\n-glued case) closes it. Over-redaction is bounded: the tokens come only from
170	        # the verified operator name and the corpus contains no unrelated bearer of these tokens
171	        # (all 256 "[REDACTED]" contexts were sampled — every one is an operator governance reference).
172	        for t in toks:
173	            if len(t) >= 4 and t.lower() not in seen:
174	                seen.add(t.lower())
175	                pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(t) + r"\b", re.IGNORECASE))
176	    return pats
177	
178	
179	def _repo_handle_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
180	    """Redactions for the operator's repo owner / GitHub handle (#J-008).
181	
182	    The handle (the ``<owner>`` in ``github.com/<owner>/<repo>``, e.g. in PR/issue URLs and
183	    ``gh --repo <owner>/...`` commands) is operator-identifying PII that NO existing rule covers:
184	    it is not an email (the email rule needs an ``@``), and it differs from the home-dir username
185	    the bare-username rule derives. Left uncovered it leaked across hundreds of transcript files
186	    (#J-008). Derive candidate handles from the git remotes and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES`` env;
187	    redact each as a bare token (length-guarded). A handle is a single unambiguous string, so no
188	    word-boundary is needed — redact it inside URLs, ``gh`` args, and the ``<handle>@`` email stem.
189	    Matched case-insensitively, and the distinctive STEM (handle minus a generic ``the`` prefix) is
190	    also redacted, because the handle leaks via derived names — e.g. ``[REDACTED]-audience-profiles``
191	    (a skill name) embeds the handle minus ``the`` (#J-008 confirm-concern).
192	    """
193	    handles: list[str] = []
194	    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES", "")
195	    handles.extend(h.strip() for h in extra.split(",") if h.strip())
196	    try:
197	        import subprocess
198	
199	        out = subprocess.run(["git", "remote", "-v"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
200	        if out.returncode == 0:
201	            for m in re.finditer(r"github\.com[:/]([^/\s]+)/", out.stdout):
202	                handles.append(m.group(1))
203	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
204	        pass
205	    # Expand each handle with its distinctive stem (drop a leading generic "the") so handle-derived
206	    # names/slugs are covered too (#J-008 confirm-concern).
207	    expanded: list[str] = []
208	    for h in handles:
209	        expanded.append(h)
210	        if h.lower().startswith("the") and len(h) - 3 >= 4:
211	            expanded.append(h[3:])
212	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
213	    seen: set[str] = set()
214	    for h in expanded:
215	        key = h.lower()
216	        if len(h) < 4 or key in seen:
217	            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
218	        seen.add(key)
219	        if len(h) >= 8:
220	            # SEPARATOR-FLEXIBLE (#J-008 confirm-concern round 3): a GitHub handle is usually a
221	            # de-spaced brand ("[REDACTED]" = "[REDACTED]"); the SPACED source brand
222	            # appears in prose/links and lets a reader reconstruct the redacted handle/email by
223	            # concatenation. Allow punctuation/whitespace between each character so the spaced,
224	            # hyphenated, and dotted forms all redact — while the de-spaced form (empty separators)
225	            # still matches. The separator class is space/._- ONLY, so unrelated prose cannot
226	            # accidentally spell out the handle, and common single tokens (e.g. "CISO") are NOT
227	            # redacted because the WHOLE letter sequence (…[REDACTED]) must be present in order.
228	            pats.append(re.compile(r"[\s._-]*".join(re.escape(c) for c in h), re.IGNORECASE))
229	        else:
230	            pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(h), re.IGNORECASE))
231	    return pats
232	
233	
234	def _hostname_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
235	    """Redactions for the operator's machine/infrastructure hostname (#P9-MO/P9-PII-1).
236	
237	    The scrubber masked email/username/display-name/handle but had NO rule for the host the node
238	    runs on, so the production machine's hostname (e.g. ``[REDACTED]`` / ``[REDACTED]``) and
239	    internal ``*.local`` mDNS/namespace hosts leaked into committed transcripts — operator/infra
240	    identity. Derive the MACHINE hostname from ``socket.gethostname``/``platform.node`` (and, on
241	    macOS, ``scutil --get LocalHostName``/``ComputerName``) and redact both its bare and ``.local``
242	    forms (a machine name is distinctive). Additional internal hosts (e.g. an RDF-namespace host
243	    like ``[REDACTED]``) are supplied via the comma-separated ``ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS`` env and have
244	    ONLY their ``.local`` form redacted — the bare label (often a project name like ``hc-grc``)
245	    appears in legitimate paths/repo refs and must not be over-redacted.
246	    """
247	    machine: list[str] = []
248	    try:
249	        machine.append(socket.gethostname())
250	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
251	        pass
252	    try:
253	        machine.append(platform.node())
254	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
255	        pass
256	    try:
257	        import subprocess
258	
259	        for arg in ("LocalHostName", "ComputerName"):
260	            out = subprocess.run(["scutil", "--get", arg], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
261	            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
262	                machine.append(out.stdout.strip())
263	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - scutil absent (non-macOS) must never break capture
264	        pass
265	    extra = [h.strip() for h in os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS", "").split(",") if h.strip()]
266	
267	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
268	    seen: set[str] = set()
269	
270	    def _base(name: str) -> str:
271	        n = name.strip()
272	        return n[: -len(".local")] if n.lower().endswith(".local") else n
273	
274	    for name in machine:  # machine hostname → redact BARE + .local (distinctive identity)
275	        b = _base(name)
276	        if len(b) < 4 or b.lower() in seen:
277	            continue
278	        seen.add(b.lower())
279	        # Leading \b (#P10-PII-2): without it the bare hostname matches as a SUBSTRING, so a short/
280	        # common derived hostname (e.g. "host" on a CI runner) would corrupt legit content — "host"
281	        # inside "localhost" → "local[REDACTED]". The boundary requires a token start.
282	        pats.append(re.compile(r"\b" + re.escape(b) + r"(?:\.local)?\b", re.IGNORECASE))
283	    for name in extra:  # supplied namespace hosts → redact ONLY the .local FQDN form
284	        b = _base(name)
285	        if len(b) < 4 or ("local:" + b.lower()) in seen:
286	            continue
287	        seen.add("local:" + b.lower())
288	        pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"\.local\b", re.IGNORECASE))
289	    return pats
290	
291	
292	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
293	_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
294	_REPO_HANDLE_RES = _repo_handle_patterns()
295	_HOSTNAME_RES = _hostname_patterns()
296	
297	
298	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
299	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
300	        if pat.groups == 2:
301	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
302	        elif pat.groups == 1:
303	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
304	        else:
305	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
306	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
307	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
308	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
309	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
310	    # Redact the operator's full display name + bare name tokens too (#J-002) — distinct PII the
311	    # username/email rules leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
312	    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
313	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
314	    # Redact the repo owner / GitHub handle (#J-008) — operator-identifying PII in PR/issue URLs
315	    # and `gh --repo <handle>/...` commands that no email/username rule covers.
316	    for pat in _REPO_HANDLE_RES:
317	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
318	    # Redact the machine/infrastructure hostname (#P9-PII-1) — the production node's hostname and
319	    # internal *.local hosts that no other rule covers.
320	    for pat in _HOSTNAME_RES:
321	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
322	    return text
323	
324	
325	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
326	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
327	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
328	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
329	
330	
331	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
332	    try:
333	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
334	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
335	        # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
336	        # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
337	        # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
338	        #
339	        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
340	        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
341	        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
342	        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
343	        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
344	        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
345	        # present) is copied verbatim.
346	        if b"\x00" in raw:
347	            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
348	        else:
349	            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
350	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
351	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
352	        else:
353	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
354	        return {
355	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
356	            "bytes": len(data),
357	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
358	        }
359	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
360	        return None
361	
362	
363	def main() -> int:
364	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
365	    if not src.is_dir():
366	        return 0
367	    session = _active_session(src)
368	    if session is None:
369	        return 0
370	    sid = session.stem
371	    captured: list[dict] = []
372	
373	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
374	    if rec:
375	        captured.append(rec)
376	
377	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
378	    subtree = src / sid
379	    if subtree.is_dir():
380	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
381	            if f.is_file():
382	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
383	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
384	                if rec:
385	                    captured.append(rec)
386	
387	    try:
388	        MANIFEST.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
389	        MANIFEST.write_text(
390	            json.dumps(
391	                {
392	                    "captured_at": _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
393	                    "active_session": sid,
394	                    "n_files": len(captured),
395	                    "total_bytes": sum(c["bytes"] for c in captured),
396	                    "files": captured,
397	                },
398	                indent=2,
399	            )
400	            + "\n",
401	            encoding="utf-8",
402	        )
403	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
404	        return 0
405	    print(f"captured {len(captured)} transcript file(s) for session {sid}")
406	    return 0
407	
408	
409	if __name__ == "__main__":
410	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""

113	def _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records: list[ClaimRecord]) -> str:

117	    engine's authoritative fingerprint and the scheme adapters must implement in

119	    over the materialized records (in scored order) and enforces agreement with the source's
120	    self-report, so an auditor re-deriving the fingerprint from the scored claims gets the value

126	def _require_unique_ids(ids: list[str], what: str) -> None:
127	    """Raise FirewallViolation if ``ids`` contains a duplicate (#138).
128	
129	    Identity (claim↔hypothesis↔verdict) must be unique within a run: a repeated id would
130	    otherwise be counted once per occurrence, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing
131	    alignment_rate / verdict accounting.
132	    """
133	    seen: set[str] = set()
134	    dupes: list[str] = []
135	    for i in ids:
136	        if i in seen:
137	            dupes.append(i)
138	        seen.add(i)
139	    if dupes:
140	        raise FirewallViolation(
141	            f"duplicate {what} within one run: {sorted(set(dupes))[:3]} — identity must be "
142	            "unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator / verdict accounting)"
143	        )

146	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
147	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
148	
149	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
150	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
151	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
152	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
153	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
154	    """
155	    return GateDecision(
156	        approved=True,
157	        gate=review.gate,
158	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
159	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
160	    )

262	def run_study(
263	    plan: StudyPlan,
264	    *,
265	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
266	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
267	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
268	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
269	    clock: Clock | None = None,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
272	    verify_trail: bool = True,
273	) -> StudyResult:
274	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
275	
276	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
277	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
278	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
279	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
280	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
281	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
282	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
283	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
284	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
285	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
286	
287	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
288	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
289	    """
290	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
291	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
292	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
293	    if secret is None and trail is None:
294	        # An engine-created unkeyed trail is tamper-evident (a naive edit breaks the SHA-256
295	        # chain) but NOT forgery-resistant: an adversary with write access can edit an entry and
296	        # recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain. That is fine for local reproducibility but
297	        # insufficient for an audit trail that crosses a trust boundary. Flag the insecure
298	        # default rather than letting it pass silently (pass 3, #F-049); pass a `secret` to key
299	        # the trail with HMAC, or `clock=`-driven deterministic repro if forgery is out of scope.
300	        warnings.warn(
301	            "run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED (secret=None) — tamper-evident but not "
302	            "forgery-resistant. Pass secret=<bytes> to HMAC-key the trail for audit trails that "
303	            "cross a trust boundary.",
304	            stacklevel=2,
305	        )
306	    defn = plan.definition
307	    modes = defn.modes
308	    required = required_phases(modes)
309	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
310	    visited: list[Phase] = []
311	    run_id: str | None = None
312	
313	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
314	        visited.append(phase)
315	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
316	
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	            # Emit a structured warning too (#K-OBS-2): a tracker call failing used to be recorded
326	            # ONLY to the trail, so e.g. a provenance-artifact serialization failure dropped the
327	            # #G-009 artifact silently — an operator auditing the run saw no artifact and no reason.
328	            _log.warning(
329	                "experiment tracker call failed (%s: %s); this metric/artifact was not logged",
330	                type(exc).__name__,
331	                exc,
332	            )
333	
334	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
335	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
336	        decision = gate_handler(review)
337	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
338	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
339	        trail.record(
340	            "gate",
341	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
342	            gate=review.gate,
343	            approved=approved,
344	            reason=reason,
345	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
346	        )
347	        if not approved:
348	            raise GateError(
349	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
350	            )
351	
352	    trail.record(
353	        "run_start",
354	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
355	        study=defn.name,
356	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
357	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
358	    )
359	    if tracker is not None:
360	        try:
361	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
362	                defn.name,
363	                {
364	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
365	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
366	                },
367	            )
368	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
369	            # run_id stays None → every track() call silently no-ops and ALL metrics are dropped
370	            # for the whole run. Record it to the trail AND emit a structured warning so an
371	            # operator seeing no MLflow run knows why instead of guessing (pass 3, #F-028).
372	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
373	            _log.warning(
374	                "experiment tracker start_run failed (%s: %s); run metrics will not be logged "
375	                "to the tracker for this study",
376	                type(exc).__name__,
377	                exc,
378	            )
379	
380	    ok = False
381	    started_at = time.monotonic()
382	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
383	    try:
384	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
385	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
386	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
387	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
388	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
389	        bootstrap_tracing()
390	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
391	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
392	        # ---- INGEST ----
393	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
394	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
395	            try:
396	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
397	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
398	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
399	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
400	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
401	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
402	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
403	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
404	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
405	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
406	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):

1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def is_local_socket_path(host: str | None) -> bool:
42	    """True for a libpq Unix-domain-socket host — a leading-'/' absolute directory (#K-SEC-1).
43	
44	    libpq accepts ``host=/var/run/postgresql`` (or the query form ``?host=/tmp``) to connect over
45	    a local Unix-domain socket — a connection that NEVER touches the network, so it is strictly
46	    more data-sovereign than the TCP loopback the guard already permits. A single leading '/' is a
47	    local path; a Windows UNC prefix (two leading separators, e.g. ``//server`` or ``/\\server``)
48	    resolves to a remote SMB share and must still be rejected.
49	    """
50	    if not host:
51	        return False
52	    h = host.strip()
53	    # A comma means libpq MULTI-HOST (e.g. "/tmp,evil.com") — never a single socket dir. Reject so
54	    # the leading-'/' check can't wave through an off-box host hidden after the comma (#K-SEC-9-1).
55	    # Callers split multi-host and validate each element; a leaf reaching here must be one host.
56	    if "," in h:
57	        return False
58	    return h.startswith("/") and not h.startswith("//") and not h.startswith("/\\")
59	
60	
61	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
62	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
63	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
64	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
65	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
66	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
67	            f"from {url!r}"
68	        )
69	    return url
70	
71	
72	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
73	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
74	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
75	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
76	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
77	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
78	        )
79	    return host
80	
81	
82	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
83	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
84	
85	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority

89	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
90	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
91	    ``[REDACTED]:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
92	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
93	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.

96	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]

99	        p = part.strip()
100	        if not p:
101	            continue
102	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
103	            end = p.find("]")
104	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
105	        else:
106	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)

111	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
112

114	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
115	    reports, since the two can disagree:

117	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
118	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host

120	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.

123	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
124	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.

131	        query = parsed.query

134	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(

136	        ) from None

140	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
141	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
142	        if "\\" in netloc:
143	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
144	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
145	            )
146	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
147	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
148	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
149	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
150	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
151	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
152	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
153	                )
154	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
155	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
156	                # libpq allows a COMMA-SEPARATED multi-host value; split and validate EVERY element
157	                # (#K-SEC-9-1) — else "/tmp,evil.com" slipped through because the whole string
158	                # looked like a socket path and the off-box host after the comma was never checked.
159	                for h in (p.strip() for p in val.split(",")):
160	                    if h and not is_loopback_host(h) and not is_local_socket_path(h):
161	                        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
162	                            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
163	                            f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
164	                        )
165	        # libpq ``service=`` URI query param loads ~/.pg_service.conf which can redirect the
166	        # connection off-box regardless of host= (#SEC-10-1). ``servicefile`` is not a real
167	        # libpq DSN keyword (the service file path is controlled by the PGSERVICEFILE env var,
168	        # blocked separately in _assert_local_libpq_env) — it is included here as belt-and-
169	        # suspenders in case a future libpq version or wrapper library adds support for it.
170	        for key in ("service", "servicefile"):
171	            if parse_qs(query).get(key):
172	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
173	                    f"{what}: URI query parameter {key!r} would load an off-box service "
174	                    "definition (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); use explicit host=localhost instead"
175	                )
176	        return uri

178	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
179	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
180	            kw = match.group(1).strip().lower()
181	            # libpq ``service=`` DSN keyword loads ~/.pg_service.conf which can redirect the
182	            # connection off-box regardless of host= (#SEC-10-1). ``servicefile`` is not a real
183	            # libpq DSN keyword — included as belt-and-suspenders (see URI query branch above).
184	            if kw in {"service", "servicefile"}:
185	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
186	                    f"{what}: DSN keyword {kw!r} would load an off-box service definition "
187	                    "(ADR-0003 data sovereignty); use explicit host=localhost instead"
188	                )
189	            if kw in {"host", "hostaddr"}:
190	                raw = match.group(2).strip().strip("'\"")
191	                # Split libpq's comma-separated multi-host and validate each element (#K-SEC-9-1).
192	                for h in (p.strip() for p in raw.split(",")):
193	                    if h and not is_loopback_host(h) and not is_local_socket_path(h):
194	                        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
195	                            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); DSN names "
196	                            f"non-loopback host {h!r}"
197	                        )
198	        return uri
199	
200	    stripped = uri.lstrip()
201	    # Windows treats ANY two leading separators (in any mix of '\' and '/') as a UNC prefix
202	    # to a remote SMB share — \\srv, //srv, /\srv, \/srv all resolve off-box (audit #D6).

204	    # here too keeps the branch self-contained.)
205	    if len(stripped) >= 2 and stripped[0] in "\\/" and stripped[1] in "\\/":
206	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
207	            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); UNC-style path "
208	            f"{uri!r} is a remote share"
209	        )
210	    return uri  # bare path / dir store
